canonical: How do I register for courses and exams at the University of Vienna?
paraphrases: What are the steps to sign up for a course or an examination?
Where do I actually enrol in my lectures and exams once I'm admitted?
Can you walk me through the course and exam registration process?
If I want to book a place in a seminar and its exam, what do I do?
weird_framing: ok so i'm finally admitted, now like how do i even get into the classes... is it in that uspace thing or ufind or
key_fact: You register for courses and exams via u:space (you are forwarded there from u:find), and you need an active u:account plus a valid admission to a degree programme. As long as you register within the registration period, the exact moment you register does not matter — there is no 'first come, first served'; places are allocated only after the deadline.

canonical: What is the STEOP and how many times can I retake its exams?
paraphrases: How many attempts do I get at the introductory and orientation period exams?
Can you explain the STEOP and what happens if I keep failing it?
What's this first-semester orientation phase, and is there a limit on retaking its tests?
If I don't pass a STEOP exam, how many more tries do I have before I'm out?
weird_framing: a friend keeps failing that first-semester intro thing... like how many shots does she actually get before they kick her off the whole programme
key_fact: Almost all bachelor, teacher-accreditation and diploma programmes require completing the STEOP (introductory and orientation period) in the first semester, and you must pass all STEOP exams before taking further courses/exams. You may retake a STEOP exam three times (four attempts total); if the fourth attempt fails, your admission to that programme expires and you are permanently excluded from it.

canonical: How many ECTS credits do I need to earn in my first four semesters?
paraphrases: What is the minimum number of credits I must complete early in my bachelor's, and by when?
Is there a required amount of ECTS I have to reach or I lose my place?
How many credit points must a new bachelor student achieve within two years?
What's the minimum study output rule for the first four semesters?
weird_framing: heard there's some rule where if you don't rack up enough credits early they boot you... how many is it and how long do i have
key_fact: In every bachelor's and diploma programme (for students admitted since winter semester 2022/23) you must obtain at least 16 ECTS credits within the first four semesters. Otherwise your admission expires and you are excluded from that programme for four semesters.

canonical: What does it mean to be 'prüfungsaktiv' (examination-active)?
paraphrases: When is a student counted as examination-active, and does it matter for me personally?
Can you explain 'prüfungsaktiv' and whether there are consequences if I'm not?
How many credits per year make me 'examination-active', and why does the university care?
What's the difference between being examination-active and just studying normally?
weird_framing: my advisor mentioned pruefungsaktiv or whatever... do i get in trouble if i'm not that, or is it just a uni money thing?
key_fact: You count as 'prüfungsaktiv' (examination-active) if you complete at least 16 ECTS credits (or at least 8 SWS) in a degree programme within one academic year. It matters for the university's funding, not for the student personally — not being examination-active has no study-law consequences for you (unlike the separate minimum-number-of-credits rule).

canonical: When does the semester start at the University of Vienna?
paraphrases: What are the start dates of the winter and summer semesters?
When do classes begin and end for the summer semester 2026?
How is the academic year split, and on what dates do the terms start?
What's the schedule of the academic year — when does teaching kick off each term?
weird_framing: planning my move to vienna lol, when does uni actually start... like the october one and the spring one, what dates
key_fact: The winter semester always begins on 1 October and the summer semester on 1 March. For summer semester 2026, classes run from 1 March to 30 June 2026, followed by the lecture-free period from 1 July to 30 September 2026.

canonical: When does course registration open for English and American Studies in summer semester 2026?
paraphrases: What are the registration phase dates for continuous-assessment courses in English and American Studies for 2026S?
For summer 2026, when can I sign up for pi-LV courses in the English and American Studies programme?
What's the registration window for English and American Studies seminars in the summer 2026 semester?
On which dates does the SPL English and American Studies open course registration for summer semester 2026?
weird_framing: anglistik people — when does the anmeldung open for the SS26 continuous-assessment courses again? like the exact start day/time
key_fact: For summer semester 2026, course registration for continuous-assessment (pi LV) courses in English and American Studies runs from 6 February 2026 at 12:00 to 20 February 2026 at 12:00. Each directorate of studies (SPL) has its own registration window, and you should always confirm individual course deadlines in u:find.

canonical: What happens if I miss the first session of a continuous-assessment course?
paraphrases: If I'm registered for a seminar (pi LV) but don't show up to the first unit, do I get removed?
Is attendance at the first meeting of a continuous-assessment course mandatory?
Can I lose my spot in an exercise course by skipping the opening session?
What are the attendance rules for courses with continuous assessment, especially the first class?
weird_framing: uhh i signed up for a seminar but might sleep through the first class... do they just yeet me off the list if i'm not there?
key_fact: Courses with continuous assessment (pi LV, e.g. seminars and exercises) have compulsory attendance; if you are registered and are absent from the first unit without a good reason, you are deregistered. Places are allocated by the point system, preference system or recommended path of study, and unused registration points cannot be carried over to the next semester.

canonical: Am I allowed to use AI tools in my coursework and exams?
paraphrases: Is using AI permitted in courses and assessments, and who decides that?
What are the rules on using AI for assignments — is it allowed by default?
When can I use AI in my studies and when is it off-limits?
How do I know whether AI is permitted for a particular course or exam?
weird_framing: real talk, can i just use chatgpt for my uni stuff or is that a no-go unless someone says it's fine?
key_fact: Whether and how you may use AI in a course or exam is determined by the lecturer, the learning outcomes and good academic practice — always check u:find, Moodle notices and the assignment instructions. If in doubt, assume AI is NOT permitted unless you have explicit permission. Submitting AI-generated work as your own or using AI in an exam without permission can count as cheating.

canonical: How do I cite and label AI that I used in my written work?
paraphrases: What's the correct way to disclose and reference AI tools in a paper?
Do I have to put ChatGPT in my bibliography, and how do I mark AI-generated text?
How should I document AI use in a thesis so it's transparent?
What information do I include when I quote text an AI produced?
weird_framing: so if i let an ai reword a paragraph... do i cite that like a normal source or what, and does it go in the references list
key_fact: You must disclose AI use transparently; failing to do so breaches the principles of good academic practice. Verbatim AI output is cited in-text with the tool name, the model, the year of use and the prompt used. AI tools should be listed in the bibliography only if a publicly accessible URL to the chat can be provided (which is usually not the case for tools like ChatGPT).

canonical: What are the consequences of using AI in an exam without permission?
paraphrases: What happens if I use an unauthorised AI tool during an examination?
Where can I check which AI tools are allowed in an exam, and what if I break the rules?
Is using AI in an exam academic misconduct, and how is it penalised?
Could I be excluded from my studies for using AI in an exam when it wasn't allowed?
weird_framing: asking hypothetically... if someone sneaks ai into an exam and gets caught, how bad is it — like just a fail or actually kicked out?
key_fact: The AI tools permitted for a particular exam are listed in the u:find course description; anything not explicitly listed there as permitted is prohibited. Using unauthorised AI in an exam is academic misconduct and leads to a non-assessment (entry of an 'X') or annulment of the grade; in serious cases the Rectorate can exclude you from studies for up to two semesters.

canonical: How much can I use the university's u:ai tool each month?
paraphrases: What monthly credit do students get for u:ai, and does leftover credit roll over?
How do I access u:ai and how much usage is included per month?
Is there a usage limit on the University of Vienna's AI platform?
What's the monthly budget for u:ai and when does it reset?
weird_framing: that uni ai thing — how much free usage do i get a month and does it stack up if i don't use it all
key_fact: u:ai is the University of Vienna's data-protection-compliant AI platform, accessed with your u:account at univie.academic-ai.at. You get a monthly credit of €20 for queries, which is automatically recharged on the first day of each month; unused credit does not carry over. Its knowledge is current only up to October 2024 unless you activate the internet search.

canonical: How do I get a confirmation of enrolment?
paraphrases: Where can I download proof that I'm a student, like a confirmation of enrolment?
How do I obtain an officially signed confirmation of my studies?
I need a document confirming I'm enrolled — where do I find it?
Can I get my student confirmations myself, or do I have to request them?
weird_framing: need to prove i'm a student for some paperwork... can i just pull that confirmation off uspace myself or do i gotta email someone
key_fact: Current students can download most confirmations themselves at any time in u:space under Personal matters > My documents (e.g. confirmation of enrolment, student record sheet); these carry an official electronic signature and are accepted by authorities. For confirmations with a 'wet' stamp or content not available in u:space, contact the Servicedesk.

canonical: How do I update my personal details, like my address or name, at the university?
paraphrases: Where can I change my address, phone number or bank details as a student?
My name changed after marriage — how do I get it updated in the university system?
Which personal data can I edit myself, and which changes need documentation?
How do I correct or change my personal information at the University of Vienna?
weird_framing: just moved flats and also got married lol — can i fix my address myself in uspace but the name change needs papers right?
key_fact: You can change some data yourself in u:space (forwarding email address, phone number, home/delivery address, bank details). A change of name requires the contact form (Topic: Change of data) to the Admission Office with documentation (e.g. a marriage certificate) and a scan of your passport or ID card.

canonical: How do I sign up to get emergency alerts about incidents at the university?
paraphrases: Is there an emergency notification service for students, and how do I register?
How can I be warned by text message if something serious happens on campus?
Where do I opt in to the university's emergency SMS alerts?
Can I get notified about emergencies like a fire at a university location?
weird_framing: does uni vienna text you if like a building's on fire or something? how do i turn that on
key_fact: Students can voluntarily register for the emergency text-message service with their private phone number in u:space; it notifies them by SMS about a sudden emergency (such as a major fire) at a University of Vienna location, and they can deregister at any time.

canonical: Where can I find vegetarian or vegan food at the University of Vienna?
paraphrases: Are there cafeterias with vegan and vegetarian meals on the university's premises?
Which locations serve plant-based food to students?
I'm vegan — where can I eat at the University of Vienna?
Does the university offer daily vegetarian or vegan dishes anywhere?
weird_framing: hungry and plant-based lol, is there anywhere at uni that does veggie/vegan stuff every day or
key_fact: The University of Vienna offers vegetarian and vegan dishes daily at two locations: the Lia Cafeteria on Oskar-Morgenstern-Platz and the Lia Bistro in the University Biology Building.

canonical: What are the basic requirements to be admitted to a master's programme at the University of Vienna?
paraphrases: What do I need in order to get into a master programme here?
Which prior qualification is required for admission to a master's programme?
Can students from other universities also study a single semester in a master programme here?
What are the general prerequisites for master admission, and is there a visiting option?
weird_framing: finished my bachelor elsewhere — what do i actually need to get into a master here, and can someone just do one semester as a guest?
key_fact: To be admitted to a master programme you must have completed an eligible bachelor/diploma programme in the same discipline; some master programmes additionally have qualitative admission requirements (QZB) or a selection procedure (AV). Master students from other universities can also enrol for one semester as 'Visiting Master' students in an eligible programme.

canonical: When is the application deadline to apply for a degree programme for winter semester 2026/27?
paraphrases: What is the application period for the winter semester 2026/27, and does it differ by citizenship?
By when do EU and non-EU applicants have to apply for winter 2026/27?
If I miss the application deadline, is there any grace period?
What are the application dates for a bachelor or master starting in winter 2026/27?
weird_framing: trying to apply for this coming winter term... whats the last day, and is it earlier if you're from outside the EU?
key_fact: For winter semester 2026/27 the general application period for EU/EEA/CH citizens is 22 June to 5 September 2026; for third-country nationals it ends earlier, on 3 August 2026. There is no grace period — if you miss the application period you must wait for the next semester.

canonical: What English test scores do I need to prove B2 proficiency for admission?
paraphrases: Which TOEFL or IELTS results count as proof of English at level B2?
How many TOEFL points or what IELTS band do I need for an English-taught programme?
What are the accepted English certificate scores for B2, and how old can they be?
For admission requiring English B2, what test results are recognised?
weird_framing: do they take my ielts for the english requirement? like what band is enough for b2 and is my old certificate too old
key_fact: For programmes requiring English at level B2, accepted test certificates include TOEFL iBT with at least 94 points or IELTS Academic with an overall band score of 6.5. Such test certificates may not be older than three years at the time of application.

canonical: What is a leave of absence and do I still have to pay fees during it?
paraphrases: If I take a leave of absence, am I exempt from the tuition and Students' Union fees?
For what reasons can I get a leave of absence, and what does it cost me?
Does taking a semester off pause my fees and my semester count?
How does a leave of absence affect my payments and my study progress?
weird_framing: thinking of taking a semester off for family stuff — do i still gotta pay anything or does everything just freeze
key_fact: A leave of absence is only granted for specific defined reasons (e.g. pregnancy, childcare, care of relatives, illness, military/civil service) and applies to all your studies. It exempts you from the tuition fee but NOT the Students' Union fee, which you must still pay on time or your studies are automatically closed. Semesters on leave are not counted (including for the minimum number of credits).

canonical: I study at another Austrian university — how can I take individual courses at the University of Vienna?
paraphrases: What is co-registration and how do I apply for it?
Can I complete single courses here and have them recognised at my home university?
How does a student from another Austrian university take a course at Uni Vienna?
What do I need to co-register for courses each semester?
weird_framing: i'm enrolled at another uni in austria but want one course at vienna and count it back home — is that the mitbelegung thing? how
key_fact: If you study at another Austrian university, you can take individual courses at the University of Vienna through co-registration (Mitbelegung), applied for each semester with a pre-approval form, and have them recognised at your home university. You cannot complete a full degree as a co-registered student, and your u:card serves only as a library card, not a student ID.

canonical: How do I switch to a different bachelor programme, and when does the change take effect?
paraphrases: What are the steps to change my bachelor or diploma programme?
If I want to change degree programmes, do I need to do an Online Self-Assessment first?
When does a change of degree programme actually become active?
How does changing to another bachelor programme work at the University of Vienna?
weird_framing: wanna jump from my current bachelor to a different one... do i have to redo that OSA thing and when does the switch actually kick in?
key_fact: To change your bachelor/diploma programme you apply for admission to the new programme within the application period; for programmes with a compulsory Online-Self-Assessment (OSA) you must first complete the OSA and enter your OSA code. The admission to the new programme only takes effect from the start of the semester (1 October or 1 March).

canonical: I'm from a non-EU country — what documents do I need to apply for a bachelor programme?
paraphrases: As a third-country applicant, which documents must I submit for a bachelor's admission?
What paperwork (certificates, legalisation, translations) do non-EU/EEA students need to apply?
Do I need my secondary-school certificate legalised and translated to apply from outside the EU?
What are the general document requirements for third-country students applying to a bachelor programme?
weird_framing: applying from outside europe, kinda lost — what docs do i actually need, like does my school certificate need that apostille/legalisation and a translation?
key_fact: Non-EU/EEA applicants need, among other documents, a passport/ID scan and all pages of the secondary-school leaving certificate with the legalisation required for the issuing country plus a sworn translation if it is not in German or English; German or multilingual programmes also require proof of German at level A2 or higher. Several countries additionally require proof of the national university entrance exam (e.g. China's Gaokao) with an Apostille.

canonical: Do I need Latin for my degree programme, and how do I prove it?
paraphrases: Which programmes require an additional exam in Latin, and how can I fulfil it?
How do I meet the Latin requirement — does school Latin count?
For programmes like Law or Classical Philology, what's the Latin qualification rule?
If a programme needs Latin, when do I have to pass it and how is it proven?
weird_framing: do i seriously need latin for law?? and if i did latin in school does that just count or do i have to sit some extra exam
key_fact: Some programmes require an additional qualification in Latin (e.g. Law, Classical Philology, History, Pharmacy). If you attended Latin at secondary school for at least ten hours per week, the knowledge is taken as given via your school transcripts; otherwise you must pass an additional exam, either before admission or during the programme (but it may not be your last exam).

canonical: How do I quit or de-register from my studies at the University of Vienna?
paraphrases: What's the process to close a degree programme and withdraw from the university?
If I want to end my studies, what steps do I need to take?
How do I formally exmatriculate from a programme, and can I do it any time of year?
What do I have to sort out (courses, library) before I can close my studies?
weird_framing: done with this programme, how do i actually bail out officially — is not paying the fee enough or do i have to do something
key_fact: You can close (formerly 'exmatriculation') a degree programme year-round via the Servicedesk after deregistering from all continuous-assessment courses and exams and returning any library books; you then receive a confirmation of withdrawal. Simply not paying the tuition/Students' Union fee also automatically closes your studies.

canonical: Which bachelor programmes at the University of Vienna are taught in English?
paraphrases: Are there any English-language bachelor's programmes here?
Can I study a bachelor programme in English at the University of Vienna?
Which undergraduate programmes are offered in English or bilingually?
What are the English-taught bachelor options at Uni Vienna?
weird_framing: my german is rough — is there literally any bachelor here i can do in english, or is it all german?
key_fact: The University of Vienna offers only two English-language/bilingual bachelor programmes: International Legal Studies (with an entrance exam procedure) and Mathematical Foundations of Data Science (with an aptitude assessment procedure). All other bachelor/diploma programmes are taught in German.

canonical: How can I check whether my bachelor's degree qualifies me for a particular master programme?
paraphrases: Where do I find out if my previous degree is eligible for a specific master?
What is the Master Access Guide and what does it tell me?
How do I know if I meet the admission requirements for a given master programme?
Is there a tool that shows which bachelor degrees give access to a master programme?
weird_framing: not sure if my bachelor even counts for the master i want... is there some guide that just tells you if you qualify or not?
key_fact: The Master Access Guide (MAG) tells you which previous bachelor/diploma programmes count as eligible for a given master, whether qualitative admission requirements (QZB) apply, and whether supplementary exams are needed. If your degree is not listed, eligibility is decided case-by-case based on a complete application — no preliminary assessment is possible.

canonical: What language are programmes taught in at the University of Vienna, and is my language level checked at admission?
paraphrases: Is the default language of instruction German, and are some programmes in other languages?
For language requirements, when is my proficiency an actual admission requirement versus just a recommendation?
Do all programmes require German, or are there English and multilingual ones too?
Will the university verify my language skills when I apply, or only recommend a level?
weird_framing: so is everything in german here by default? and do they actually check my language level when i apply or is it just a 'nice to have'
key_fact: The general language of instruction at the University of Vienna is German, though some programmes are taught in English or in a German-plus-other-language combination. For some programmes the required language level is a binding admission requirement (you must upload a certificate), while for others the stated level is only a recommendation and is not checked during the admission process.

canonical: How big is the University of Vienna and how many degree programmes does it offer?
paraphrases: How many students and study programmes does the University of Vienna have?
What's the size of the University of Vienna in terms of students and programmes?
How many degree programmes, including master's, are on offer, and how old is the university?
Give me the key figures on the University of Vienna — students, programmes, history.
weird_framing: how huge is this place actually... like how many students, how many programmes, and is it really super old?
key_fact: The University of Vienna is the largest and most diverse educational institution in Austria, with about 90,000 students, 178 degree programmes (including more than 110 master's programmes) and students from around 140 nations. It is 650 years old, the oldest university in the German-speaking world.

canonical: How can I figure out whether a degree programme is right for me before I apply?
paraphrases: What tools help me decide which study programme suits me?
Is there a self-assessment I can take to get a feel for a programme?
How do I orient myself when choosing a degree and career direction?
Where can I get feedback on whether a programme fits my interests and abilities?
weird_framing: honestly no clue what to study... is there like a quiz or virtual try-out that tells me if a programme fits before i commit?
key_fact: To help you decide whether a programme fits you, the University offers voluntary Online Self-Assessments (OSAs, e.g. via uni:check) that give an insight into the degree programme and your own abilities and provide individual feedback and advice; additional counselling services and interest tests are also available.

canonical: What is an extension curriculum (EC) and how many ECTS does it involve?
paraphrases: Can you explain extension curricula and their credit size?
What are ECs in a bachelor's programme, and when can I start taking their courses?
How many ECTS is an extension curriculum, and is there an alternative to it?
Do I have to do an extension curriculum, and how big is it?
weird_framing: whats this erweiterungscurriculum thing everyone in my bachelor has to do... how many ects and can i only start after steop?
key_fact: An extension curriculum (EC) is a predefined module group of up to 30 ECTS credits that almost all bachelor's programmes require in addition to the main subject; you register for the EC in u:space and can take its individual courses only after passing the STEOP. Instead of an EC you may, in most bachelor's programmes, complete alternative extensions (AE) of at least 15 ECTS.

canonical: Where can I do professional continuing education or a postgraduate programme at the University of Vienna?
paraphrases: Does the University of Vienna offer continuing education and certificate programmes for professionals?
What is the Postgraduate Center and what kinds of programmes does it run?
I want lifelong-learning or a professional master — where do I look?
Which fields does the university's continuing-education arm cover?
weird_framing: i'm working already but want some professional course or exec master from uni vienna — is there a special centre for that kinda thing?
key_fact: The Postgraduate Center is the University of Vienna's competence center for professional continuing education and lifelong learning, offering continuing-education programmes, certificate programmes and master's programmes (around 70 programmes) across fields such as Law, Psychotherapy, Pharmacy and Health, and Environment and Sustainability.

canonical: I have a disability — can I take my exams in a different format?
paraphrases: Am I entitled to an alternative mode of assessment because of my impairment?
How do I arrange a modified exam format to compensate for a disability?
What is an alternative mode of assessment and how do I apply for one?
Can exams be adapted for students with impairments, and what's the procedure?
weird_framing: got adhd and standard exams wreck me — can they let me do it differently, and do i have to file something with the SPL or just ask the teacher?
key_fact: Students with a study-relevant impairment have a legal right to an alternative mode of assessment (same content and required performance, but a different/flexible mode). You arrange it either by directly agreeing with the lecturer/examiner or by formal application to the directorate of studies (SPL) with a specialist medical certificate. A granted application does not notify teachers automatically — you must inform each lecturer yourself.

canonical: How can I get advice about studying with a disability at the University of Vienna?
paraphrases: Who do I contact for counselling on accessible studying?
Is there a disability advisory team, and how do I reach them?
How do I book a consultation with the Accessible Studying Team?
Where can students with impairments get information and support?
weird_framing: need to talk to someone about accessible studying stuff — is there a team for that and when's their walk-in / how do i email them
key_fact: The Team Barrierefrei (Accessible Studying Team) advises students and prospective students with disabilities on all aspects of accessible studying. You reach them by email at barrierefrei@univie.ac.at, and personal consultation appointments are available on Wednesdays between 1pm and 5pm by prior arrangement.

canonical: As an incoming student with a disability, can I get personal assistance at the University of Vienna?
paraphrases: Does the university provide personal assistance for exchange students with disabilities?
Is there support like the ISU project for incoming students with impairments?
How can an incoming Erasmus student with a disability get study support here?
What personal-support options exist for incoming disabled students, and do I have to apply?
weird_framing: coming on erasmus and i have a disability — does the uni give you a personal assistant or is there some student-buddy support thing i need to sign up for early?
key_fact: The University of Vienna does not itself provide personal assistance for incoming students with disabilities; however, the Accessible Studying Team coordinates the 'ISU' project, in which teacher-education students support students with disabilities or chronic illnesses, and you have to apply for it in advance.

canonical: When are the application deadline and the test for programmes with an entrance exam in 2026/27?
paraphrases: For programmes with an entrance exam procedure, when do I apply and when is the written test?
What are the key dates for the 2026/27 entrance exam procedure?
When does the entrance-exam application period close and when do the tests take place?
Is there only one entrance-exam round per year, and when are the tests held?
weird_framing: applying for psych which has that entrance exam — whats the deadline and when's the actual test, and is there a backup date if i can't make it?
key_fact: For programmes with an entrance exam procedure in 2026/27, the application period is 2 March to 4 May 2026 and the written tests take place on site in Vienna from 6 July 2026 onwards. The procedure runs only once per academic year with no alternative dates, and results are sent by email about two weeks after the test.

canonical: How is the Psychology entrance test structured and how many places are awarded?
paraphrases: What parts does the Psychology bachelor written test consist of, and how are they weighted?
How many study places does the Psychology entrance exam give out?
For the Psychology admission test, what's tested and how is the ranking decided?
Can you break down the Psychology entrance exam sections and the number of available places?
weird_framing: psych entrance test — how's it split up, like what counts most, and how far down the ranking do you still get a spot?
key_fact: In the Psychology bachelor entrance exam, the written test has Part A (acquiring subject-related knowledge from literature, weighted 50%), Part B (methodical, formal and analytical thinking, 25%) and Part C (understanding simple German and English texts, 25%). Participants ranked between position 1 and 485 receive a study place.

canonical: How much is the tuition fee at the University of Vienna?
paraphrases: What do I actually pay per semester in tuition and Students' Union fees?
How much does it cost each semester for an EU student versus a non-EU student?
What are the fee amounts, and when does the tuition fee apply for EU citizens?
Do EU/EEA students pay tuition, or just the Students' Union fee?
weird_framing: asking for a friend — that uni vienna money thing per semester, ballpark? and do eu people even pay the big fee or just the small one
key_fact: EU/EEA/Switzerland citizens pay only the ~26.20 EUR Students' Union fee each semester during the stipulated programme duration plus two tolerance semesters; the ~363.36 EUR tuition fee (total ~389.56 EUR) applies only if they exceed that period. Non-EU/EEA citizens generally pay ~726.72 EUR tuition from the first semester, with some exemptions.

canonical: How and by when do I pay my tuition fee to stay enrolled?
paraphrases: What are the payment options and the payment deadline for the tuition/Students' Union fee?
How does paying the fee re-enrol me, and what happens if I miss the deadline?
By when must I pay for winter semester 2026/27, and how can I pay?
What's the process for paying the fee and re-enrolling each semester?
weird_framing: how do i pay this semester's fee and by when... can i just do it with a card in uspace and what if i'm late or pay too little
key_fact: Paying the exact prescribed fee within the payment period (winter semester 2026/27: 13 July to 31 October 2026) automatically re-enrols you. You can pay via eps or credit card in u:space (booked the same day) or by online banking/payment form (up to seven days). If the full fee is not received by the deadline, your degree programme is automatically closed; you cannot pay in advance for the next semester.

canonical: I stopped studying and want to resume the same degree programme — how does that work?
paraphrases: How do I take up my old degree programme again after interrupting it?
What's the procedure to resume the same programme, and which curriculum will I be under?
If I come back to the same programme, do I have to reapply or redo an entrance exam?
Can I simply continue my previous programme, or are there special steps for some programmes?
weird_framing: dropped out of my programme a while back, wanna come back to the same one — do i just message them or do i have to redo the whole entrance test again?
key_fact: To resume the same programme you apply within the application period (usually via the contact form) and are admitted to the current curriculum. For most programmes without an entrance exam you do not reapply in u:space or redo an Online Self-Assessment. Exceptions: the Psychology bachelor requires passing the entrance exam again, and master programmes with a selection procedure require redoing the selection procedure.

canonical: What is u:space and what can I do with it?
paraphrases: What is the u:space portal used for during my studies?
Which tasks can I handle in u:space — fees, registration, documents?
Can you explain the main functions of u:space?
How does u:space fit into organising my studies at the University of Vienna?
weird_framing: everyone keeps saying 'do it in uspace' — what even is that portal and what all can you actually do in it?
key_fact: u:space is the University of Vienna's portal for organising your studies, accessed with your u:account: you use it to apply for admission, order your u:card, pay the tuition/Students' Union fee, register for and deregister from courses and exams, check grades in your record of examinations, and download and print your study documents.
